name: Arnedo, Préjano - circular via Senda de los Cuñaos
name_original: Arnedo, Préjano – circular por Senda de los Cuñaos
region_intro: Mountain biking around Arnedo offers diverse terrain within the valley of the River Cidacos, set against the backdrop of the Sierra de Arnedo. The region features mountains and rock formations composed of conglomerates and sandstones, creating a rugged and varied landscape. Extensive trail networks provide a mix of flowy sections, varied slopes, and technical features for riders. This area is recognized for its challenging routes, attracting mountain bikers with its natural beauty and well-developed paths. Best mountain bike trails around Arnedo The most popular mountain bike trail is Arnedo, Préjano - circular via Senda de los Cuñaos, a 24.3 miles (39.2 km) trail that takes 4 hours 10 minutes to complete. This difficult route includes sections of the notable Senda de los Cuñaos. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Senda de los Cuñados – Ermita de San Marcos loop from Arnedo, a difficult 28.3 miles (45.6 km) path. This route navigates through varied terrain, offering views towards the Ermita de San Marcos. Local mountain bikers also love the Cidacos Greenway – Arnedillo and thermal baths loop from Arnedo, a 30.5 miles (49.0 km) trail leading through the scenic Cidacos River valley, often completed in about 4 hours 0 minutes. Mountain biking around Arnedo is defined by the River Cidacos valley, the imposing Sierra de Arnedo, and distinctive rock formations. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various ability levels. The routes in Arnedo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 700 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Arnedo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Arnedo?
A: Arnedo offers a wide variety of mountain biking options. Komoot lists 45 mountain bike routes, ranging from easy to difficult. Additionally, the broader Arnedo region is recognized for its extensive network, with over 200 trails listed on other popular mountain biking resources.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of mountain bike trails in Arnedo?
A: The trails around Arnedo cater to various skill levels. You'll find 4 easy routes, 25 moderate routes, and 16 difficult routes. The difficult trails, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993642975/arnedo-prejano-circular-via-senda-de-los-cunaos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arnedo, Préjano - circular via Senda de los Cuñaos&lt;/a&gt;, often require good physical fitness and cycling experience, sometimes even requiring riders to push their bikes.

Q: When is the best time of year for mountain biking in Arnedo?
A: Arnedo experiences a Mediterranean climate with hot, dry summers and mild, wet winters. Therefore, spring and autumn are generally the most ideal seasons for mountain biking, offering pleasant temperatures and favorable trail conditions.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Arnedo?
A: Yes, many routes in Arnedo are designed as circular or loop trails. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20150910" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda de los Cuñados – Ermita de San Marcos loop from Arnedo&lt;/a&gt; and the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20150744" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cidacos Greenway – Arnedillo and thermal baths loop from Arnedo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural attractions can I see while mountain biking in Arnedo?
A: The region boasts stunning natural landscapes. You can encounter impressive rock formations like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839751" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Isasa&lt;/a&gt;, the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1751438" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Autol and its Picuezos&lt;/a&gt;, and the striking &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6307839" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Red Cliffs of Herce&lt;/a&gt;. The area also features caves such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6307877" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cuevas del Ajedrezado of Santa Eulalia Somera&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical sites or castles along the mountain bike routes?
A: Yes, Arnedo and its surroundings are rich in history. You can explore sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5344963" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Préjano Castle&lt;/a&gt;, the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5344965" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Arnedo&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5060251" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Quel&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40304219" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tower of the Préjano Castle – Ermita de San Marcos loop from Arnedo&lt;/a&gt; is one route that passes by historical landmarks.

Q: Can I find thermal baths near the mountain bike trails?
A: Absolutely. Just a few kilometers from Arnedo, in Arnedillo, you can find freely accessible natural thermal baths along the Cidacos River. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20150744" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cidacos Greenway – Arnedillo and thermal baths loop from Arnedo&lt;/a&gt; is a great route that incorporates this attraction.

Q: Are there family-friendly mountain bike trails in Arnedo?
A: For families or those seeking a more relaxed ride, Arnedo offers 4 easy mountain bike routes. The Cidacos Greenway, which is part of routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20151937" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Autol and its Picuezos – Cidacos Greenway loop from Arnedo&lt;/a&gt;, provides pleasant rides with gentle gradients, suitable for various ages and skill levels.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in Arnedo?
A: The mountain biking experience in Arnedo is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 100 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, from 'flowy' sections to technical features, and the stunning natural beauty of the River Cidacos valley and Sierra de Arnedo.

Q: Are there any specific challenging downhill sections in Arnedo?
A: Yes, Arnedo is known for its challenging downhill sections. Recently developed trails like 'La Herradura' and 'El Tubo' offer exciting descents, alongside well-known trails such as 'Sacacorchos' and 'Harakiri', providing a dynamic riding experience for advanced riders.

Q: Is Arnedo a good destination for mountain biking events?
A: Arnedo has gained recognition as a premier location for mountain biking and enduro, and it regularly hosts the La Rioja BIKE RACE, an annual multi-stage mountain bike race. This highlights the quality and challenge of the region's trails.
tips: For me one of the most beautiful paths on today's route. Not excessively technical, although it does have a delicate point.
Raised on a small hillock northwest of Préjano, in La Rioja, are the remains of a 15th-century castle. In the current enclosure you can see a small tower 15 meters high.
Hermitage of San Marcos, on the one hand the ruins of the old hermitage, and on the other the new hermitage restored in 2002.
